title: Algorithmic bias in decision-making
explanation:
Algorithmic bias in decision-making

Automated systems now influence hiring, lending and policing, yet algorithms can reproduce historical inequalities if they are trained on biased data. Proponents argue that well-designed models may reduce subjective human prejudice. Critics respond that opacity makes discrimination harder to detect and challenge. Regulators increasingly demand audits, documentation and the right to explanation, though technical complexity may limit how meaningful such explanations are for ordinary citizens.

questions.text: Algorithms can reproduce inequalities when trained on [*biased data|perfect data|random art].
Proponents believe good models might reduce [*human prejudice|internet speed|museum visits].
Critics worry that opacity makes discrimination harder to [*detect|celebrate|ignore voluntarily].
Regulators want audits and the right to [*explanation|entertainment|exercise].

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: Automated systems affect hiring, lending and ____.  | *policing | gardening | cooking
Technical complexity may limit meaningful ____.  | *explanations | vacations | recipes
Critics emphasise model ____.  | *opacity | opacity only wrong | openness only
The article is [*balanced|entirely optimistic|completely dismissive of technology].
The main issue is [*fairness and accountability|fashion design|recipe quality].
Ordinary citizens may struggle to understand technical [*explanations|competition|isolation].
